Eliminated two self-referential drift sources identified in L20-L22 churn pattern. (1) docs/product/v2-master-plan.md L8 '19 step_closed rows so far' → 'append-only; see file for current count' — that line was being bumped every ledger append, generating Mx self-citation churn. (2) docs/CLAUDE-NOTES.md 'Latest L20 entry is the most recent landing' → 'Read the last entry for the most recent landing' — same pattern. Living count remains in docs/product/v2/lane-status.md (single canonical place, designed to refresh per ledger append per its own update protocol). Net effect: master-plan.md and CLAUDE-NOTES.md become count-stable; future ledger appends only touch ledger.jsonl + lane-status.md, not three files. Honesty-gate trigger: turns 4-7 each had to bump these two count fields, which generated new ledger entries needing more bumps. Solving the symptom (re-bump) was self-defeating; solving the cause (delete the count) ends the loop. 4 V2 contract harnesses still green locally (H1-H4). ai-native cppunit suite OK(77) + V1.5 baseline untouched. Production code unchanged.

Discovered W3 Day-1b prerequisite gap and recorded as D7 in handoff doc. docs/schemas/apply-plan.schema.json is the V1.5 m3-02 capability descriptor (per-capability one-time metadata: id, undo_group, failure_behavior); it is NOT the runtime ApplyPlan envelope that W3 Day-1b SwDocShell::applyDiagnosticsPlan(plan) consumes. The runtime envelope shape (plan_id / source_diagnostic_id / doc_snapshot_hash / patches[] with patch_id, kind, target paragraph_id+text_hash, before/after, severity) is described only in prose in docs/product/v2/w3-writer-apply-runtime-spec.md §'ApplyPlan 输入示例' — no JSON Schema, no fixture, no harness lock. Coordinator did not auto-design this because (a) it is a new schema-contract decision affecting D1 input shape, (b) V2 invariants memory rule #5 explicitly forbids auto-merging adjacent schemas, (c) honesty gate: schema design with downstream impact on production code is not 'reversible local doc edit'. D7 added to handoff doc blocker table; future session or user-authorized turn must own this design before W3 Day-1b wiring. Zero schema/code change this turn; only handoff doc updated. 4 V2 harness state unchanged from L24 verification.

D7 (runtime ApplyPlan schema gap) PARTIALLY RESOLVED. docs/schemas/apply-plan-runtime.schema.json (JSON Schema 2020-12, additionalProperties:false) locks the W3 Day-1b SwDocShell::applyDiagnosticsPlan() input envelope: schema_version const v2-w3-runtime-1, plan_id (^ap-...$), source_diagnostic_id (^diag-...$), doc_snapshot_hash (^sha256:[a-f0-9]{64}$), patches array (1-256 items, each with patch_id ^p[0-9]+$, kind enum 7-token, target.paragraph_id ^swpara-[0-9]+$, optional severity/rationale), preview_only bool. Per-kind internal shape (paragraph-replace before/after, paragraph-format format_changes, text-format range/format_changes, etc.) intentionally NOT locked at envelope level — each SwUndoApplyPatch impl (W3 Day-1b/c/d) lands per-kind validation alongside its production code. simplicity-first: avoid over-locking before patch impls exist. + 2 fixtures (apply-plan-runtime.{valid,invalid}.json) demonstrating 3 patch kinds (paragraph-replace + paragraph-format + text-format) and 5 distinct invalidations (BAD plan_id, non-sha256 hash, P_INVALID patch_id, bogus kind, severity 'critical' not in enum, paragraph_id missing 'swpara-' prefix). Distinct from V1.5 docs/schemas/apply-plan.schema.json (m3-02 capability descriptor: id/preview_action_id/capability_id/undo_group/failure_behavior — descriptor lives at capability registration time, not per-call). Per V2 invariants memory rule #5 schemas remain segregated. tests/v2-plan-baseline-test.sh count bump 24→26 / 10→11 + status banner. 4 V2 harnesses all green: H1=26, H2=21/26/11, H3=schema-match, H4=26/0. ai-native cppunit OK(77) + V1.5 27/27 baseline both untouched. Production C++ unchanged. D1 (sw/source wiring) still gated; this lands the fixture requirement spec §187 explicitly required.

Audit finding triggered by L28 commit-manifest verification. bin/bffvalidator.sh working-tree state had unauthorized mode-bit change 100644→100755 (file content unchanged, only chmod +x). clavue.md / coordinator-reversible scope memory explicitly lists 'do NOT touch bffvalidator.sh mode bit' — earlier session must have run chmod or staged the bit accidentally. Action: git checkout -- bin/bffvalidator.sh restored mode to 100644 per HEAD; git diff bin/bffvalidator.sh now empty (0 lines). Manifest L37 corrected: bffvalidator entry struck through with REVERTED note + explicit exclusion from any commit group. This is exactly the kind of 'silent drift before commit approval' the manifest exists to catch. No production code touched, no schema changed; only one mode-bit revert + one manifest correction + this ledger row. 4 V2 contract harnesses unaffected (none touch bffvalidator).

Audit-driven correction of stale 'D2 gated' status. Direct read of /Users/lu/kdoffice-src/kqoffice/source/ai/provider/{OllamaAdapter.hxx,OllamaAdapter.cxx,Provider.cxx} shows W1 Day-1a is in fact LANDED, not gated: OllamaAdapter.cxx implements real HTTP/1.0 (BSD sockets via socket/connect/send/recv; SO_RCVTIMEO+SO_SNDTIMEO clamped to 100ms for probe/listModels and 30s for generate; bounded reads at 8KB tags / 256KB generate; HTTP status-line guard rejects non-2xx; appendJsonEscaped sanitizes prompt for embedding). parseModelsJson + parseGenerateJson are linear-scan token extractors (no full JSON parser) handling \"/\\/\n/\t/\r/\b/\f escapes. Provider.cxx::call dispatches reachable→listModels→generate→{ok|provider-error}, falls through to provider-error on every failure path, records EvidenceRecord on every allowed call (not just success). cppunit suite includes 5 OllamaAdapter cases (testParseModelsJsonFixture, testParseGenerateJsonFixture, testParseGenerateJsonHandlesEscapes, testProbeUnreachableOnClosedPort, testProbeDoesNotHang) within OK(77). docs/product/v2/lane-status.md updated: W1 row gains explicit Day-1a entry; wave-rollup line updated to step3 + Day-1a + Day-1b; D2 in Open decisions table struck through with RESOLVED note. No code change, no schema change; only doc-level audit correction. 4 V2 contract harnesses unaffected by audit. Real takeaway: lane-status drift accumulated since session compaction — direct file read trumps memory recall (per V2 invariants memory rule on staleness). Next-up gates remain D1 (W3 Day-1b SwDocShell wiring) and D3 (W2 popover controller); D5 (downstream-branding) and D6 (worker scope) unchanged.
